learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate a critical and in-depth understanding of key topics in an area of psychology.
Demonstrate an ability to communicate orally and in writing about a topic in an area of psychology.
Demonstrate an understanding of how different types of research methods can contribute to our understanding of an area of psychology.
Effectively engage with others to discuss and evaluate empirical research.

assessments.due_date:
10/08/2026 - 26/10/2026

Presentation times and journal articles to be assigned in Week 1 of class.
assessments.task_description:
Students will each give a 15 minute presentation where they summarise and critique a peer-reviewed journal article to the class (approx. 10 minutes) and then facilitate discussion (approx. 5 minutes). Students may choose from the list of pre-approved publications. Your presentation should provide an overview of the article's background, method and findings but please do not just summarise the contents of the paper. You should demonstrate your ability for critical analysis, but evaluating the research methodology and highlighting key findings or parts of the study that are interesting (e.g., Why do these findings matter? What can we do with this information? How robust was the study and reporting, and how does this affect how we interpret the findings?). At the end of your presentation, you should identify some potential topics for the group to discuss.


Other notes:

  • You are required to email a copy of your PowerPoint Presentation to the Course Coordinator prior to the commencement of class on the day of your presentation.
  • In addition, the Course Coordinator will record the oral presentations per University policy that any live oral assessment item worth 20% or more must be recorded and retained as the official recording of the item for re-mark purposes (if required).
  • Marking criteria will be made available on Blackboard and discussed in class.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
Active participation in the weekly group discussion is critical for getting the most out of this course. Therefore, you will be expected to ask questions and discuss the material being presented in a thoughtful and constructive manner. Your participation will be assessed based on both frequency and the degree to which your contributions are respectful, considered, and relevant.

If you are unable to participate actively during the session for whatever reason, please discuss with the course coordinator ahead of time.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessments.due_date:
10/08/2026 - 26/10/2026

Submit via Blackboard before the start of each week's class.
assessments.task_description:
Starting from the third week of class, you will be required to submit a brief (i.e., approx. 150-200 words) piece of reflective work relating to ONE of the readings being covered in each week's class.

The purpose of this task is to:

  • Remain engaged with the topic of each week's class
  • Help you think of contributions to the group discussion
  • Practice critical analysis - instead of simply describing the study and its findings, you should evaluate the strengths/weaknesses and consider the implications of the paper.
  • Encourage you to think about how this paper relates to what you already know and what is new to you, and how this new information might be applicable in a real-world context.


Each week's submission will be assessed on a Pass/Fail basis. Ten journal submissions marked as "pass" are required for the full 20%.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Please submit via Blackboard prior to class each week.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
You are required to submit a 1500 (+/- 150) word written piece with an evidence-based call to action to address a novel area or current unmet need in the context of appearance and body-image. You should firstly identify what change is required, using evidence to build your argument about why the current state of affairs requires action. You should then suggest a specific avenue for change and outline what this involves. Again, you must refer to existing research to demonstrate why your solution is likely to be a feasible and effective one.

To choose your topic, think about what you've learned and discussed this Semester, what positive change can you identify and build an empirical argument to justify. You are welcome (and strongly encouraged) to discuss your topic with the course co-ordinator. Please note that personal campaigns (e.g., writing to a specific individual/group in a personal capacity) are not appropriate.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit via Turnitin on Blackboard. NOTE: You will no longer receive an automatic submission receipt from Turnitin confirming your assignment has been received. You should download a copy of the digital receipt from the Assignment Dashboard and keep this as a record.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 1 - Course introduction

Introduction to the Seminar, overview of assessment, and assigning presentation papers.
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 1 - Course introduction Introduction to the Seminar, overview of assessment, and assigning presentation papers.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 2 - Lecture

Week 2 will involve a lecture on key theoretical frameworks and a demonstration of a paper presentation.
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 2 - Lecture Week 2 will involve a lecture on key theoretical frameworks and a demonstration of a paper presentation.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 3 - Student Presentations

Presentations will commence this week and students will need to submit their reflections ahead of class. We're dedicating most of this week's class to listening to presentations, and all papers will address appearance ideals and internalisation.
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 3 - Student Presentations Presentations will commence this week and students will need to submit their reflections ahead of class. We're dedicating most of this week's class to listening to presentations, and all papers will address appearance ideals and internalisation.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 4 - Lecture and presentations

This week's topic will be male body image and traditional Western masculine gender norms.
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 4 - Lecture and presentations This week's topic will be male body image and traditional Western masculine gender norms.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 5 - Presentations and lecture

Appearance ideals and body image in sexual and gender minorities, and other under-represented populations. In this class in particular, students will be encouraged to consider how well the research in the area of body image broadly is inclusive of and addresses the needs of Indigenous Australians.
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 5 - Presentations and lecture Appearance ideals and body image in sexual and gender minorities, and other under-represented populations. In this class in particular, students will be encouraged to consider how well the research in the area of body image broadly is inclusive of and addresses the needs of Indigenous Australians.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 6 - Student presentations

This week will be primarily student presentations on the topic of social media and online spaces, with a lot of time for discussion!
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 6 - Student presentations This week will be primarily student presentations on the topic of social media and online spaces, with a lot of time for discussion!

learning_activities.topic:
Week 7 - Lecture and presentations

This will be the first of two weeks covering appearance and weight stigma. This week will provide an introduction to the topic with a focus on the impact of stigma on the individual.
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 7 - Lecture and presentations This will be the first of two weeks covering appearance and weight stigma. This week will provide an introduction to the topic with a focus on the impact of stigma on the individual.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 8 - Lecture and presentations

This will be our second week focusing on appearance and weight stigma, primarily looking at stigma in the context of healthcare.
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 8 - Lecture and presentations This will be our second week focusing on appearance and weight stigma, primarily looking at stigma in the context of healthcare.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 9 - lecture and presentations

This week will focus on cosmetic procedures/surgery and other appearance-enhancing products.
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 9 - lecture and presentations This week will focus on cosmetic procedures/surgery and other appearance-enhancing products.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 11 - Clinical Focus 1 (Lecture and presentations)

This week will focus on clinical implications of body image concerns.This includes a lecture on the assessment, conceptualisation, and treatment of eating disorders and related psychopathology. Student presentations will take place this week.
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 11 - Clinical Focus 1 (Lecture and presentations) This week will focus on clinical implications of body image concerns.This includes a lecture on the assessment, conceptualisation, and treatment of eating disorders and related psychopathology. Student presentations will take place this week.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 12 - Clinical focus 2 (Lecture and presentations)

This week will focus on clinical implications of body image concern and related outcomes. This includes assessment, conceptualisation, and treatment of eating disorders and related psychopathology.
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 12 - Clinical focus 2 (Lecture and presentations) This week will focus on clinical implications of body image concern and related outcomes. This includes assessment, conceptualisation, and treatment of eating disorders and related psychopathology.
